mnemonics: Deadlocks are like waiting rooms where processes are stuck and can't proceed
Think of a locked door where no process can enter or exit
keyPoints: A deadlock is a situation where two or more processes are blocked indefinitely
A deadlock occurs when two or more processes are competing for resources in a circular manner
The four necessary conditions for a deadlock to occur are:Mutual Exclusion, Hold and Wait, No Preemption and Priority Inversion
